title: Tokyo 3 and Trains
wikitext:
[]: Tokyo-3 and Trains             Quote Post by Hunter2458 » Tue Dec 15, 2009 5:49 am So we all know that Tokyo-3 is big and cool and techy and retractable and various other things, but there are few things it isn't. One being well referenced and organized. Structures and layouts vary from reference to reference, and there's no real map to the city. How Gainax ever figured out where they were sending people, or even how Misato found Shinji is becoming a large mystery as a Friend and I have gone about trying to recreate the city in a game called MineCraft. We've mostly been trying to base the whole thing off of two great pictures. Hakone Region Eva Style and Tokyo-3 Drafted Picture Right off the bat you can notice lots of differences just between the two, the first obviously seems to layout what the final product of Tokyo-3 would look like (or maybe what they covered up with the mountains, but unlikely.), and the Top and Bottom image of the drafted picture have many differences, and missing buildings and roads. They've mainly served their purpose until we got to the trains. Only one image really shows any connection to trains, near the west side of the lake the train enters Tokyo-3 on the map. Me and my friend both know full well there are trains running through Tokyo-3, but to what extent. Are they heavily commuting? Do they loop around the city? Is there any other means of Public Transportation that does that? Before we dive into the hours of video we will most certainly need to traverse, we were wondering, well I am wondering, if anyone knew full well the answers, or had a secret stash of maps and images that could full well solve this. As a gift, here's our first published image of what we have so far. Tokyo-3 Minecraft PS: All these images are Huge.

[]: Re: Tokyo-3 and Trains             Quote Post by Merridian » Tue Dec 15, 2009 9:03 am Hunter2458 wrote: Me and my friend both know full well there are trains running through Tokyo-3, but to what extent. Are they heavily commuting? Do they loop around the city? Is there any other means of Public Transportation that does that?

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Tue Dec 15, 2009 10:42 am Man, I used to play minecraft for hours and never made anything that big. Nice job, especially considering the available material

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Dec 15, 2009 11:20 am I'm wondering what the hell the large, flat rectangles on the ground around the edge of the city are supposed to be. Giant solar panels, maybe?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Dec 15, 2009 11:35 am Shinji wasn't in Tokyo-3 proper when Misato found him. IIRC, he was in the next town over, or the Hakone outskirts.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Dec 15, 2009 11:48 am You can work out roughly where he has to be from the road signs quoting distances, and IIRC it's somewhere centre-right in that map, between the yellow patches

[]: Re: Tokyo-3 and Trains             Quote Post by Hunter2458 » Tue Dec 15, 2009 4:48 pm Merridian wrote: a "Tokyo-3 Loop Line" is what Shinji was riding in Ep. 04, though you probably already knew that.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Dec 15, 2009 4:55 pm Wow, that picture is so good! How did you make it?

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter2458 » Tue Dec 15, 2009 4:58 pm Minecraft , it's a building and destroying game still in the alpha stages. We used the main first person building a lot, but some of the more technical aspects, like the Geofront's dome or the plates, we used a Map editor. We also drew the mountains in hand with paint, layer by layer.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Dec 15, 2009 6:04 pm An interesting project here. There's a medium sized topographical map of Tokyo-3 in this commentary cut (click the thumbnail for the medium version). I could have swon we had a larger and more detailed version somewhere about, but I can't seem to find it at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter2458 » Tue Dec 15, 2009 6:15 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: An interesting project here. There's a medium sized topographical map of Tokyo-3 in this commentary cut (click the thumbnail for the medium version). I could have swon we had a larger and more detailed version somewhere about, but I can't seem to find it at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter2458 » Sat Dec 19, 2009 3:49 pm I know I should edit my post when I'm the last reply, but it needs a bump because I have a new request. Does anyone have any tophraphical maps of the hakone region? Present or Future is great, we just can't seem to find a true topographical map with or without lables. Google Maps terrain is not a topographical map in the right sense, it's got 3D depth, but the shading is purely for looks.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Dec 19, 2009 5:32 pm Hunter2458 wrote: Does anyone have any tophraphical maps of the hakone region? Present or Future is great, we just can't seem to find a true topographical map with or without lables. Google Maps terrain is not a topographical map in the right sense, it's got 3D depth, but the shading is purely for looks.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter2458 » Sat Dec 19, 2009 5:41 pm Ornette wrote: Hunter2458 wrote: Does anyone have any topographical maps of the hakone region? Present or Future is great, we just can't seem to find a true topographical map with or without lables. Google Maps terrain is not a topographical map in the right sense, it's got 3D depth, but the shading is purely for looks.
